{{nihongo|'''Tenmansha'''|天満社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tenmansha'''|天満社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 天満社・鹽竈神社 |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
天満社 |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}550番地1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''天満社'''
{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''鹽竈神社'''
{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||塩土老翁}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（天満社） |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 北野天神・北野天満宮・天神さんなど |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}}付近（天満社）{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = {{jalink|7月26日|Q2723}}付近（鹽竈神社） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=天満社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''天満社'''（てんまんしゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}にある神社。{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}の地名の由来になっている。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、ここでは末社の{{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753|塩竃神社}}も説明する。 === 歴史 === *創建は不明、{{jalink|横山党|Q11542517}}の一族が{{jalink|北野天満宮|Q662176|京都北野天満宮}}を{{jalink|勧請}}したとされている『八王子長沼町・北野町わが街』 清水正之著 温故堂 1987年発行『八王子辞典』 八王子辞典の会 かたくら書店 2001年発行。 *{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}（{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年）〜{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}（{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年）5石の{{jalink|社領}}を拝領する{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}将軍に武運長久の祈願札を毎年献上していた{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}ご神木である{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}が植えられる。 *{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年）社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}13年）{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}向拝の社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）{{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}}周辺の区画整理により{{jalink|八王子市立由井第一小学校|Q11391597|由井第一小学校}}内にあった北野石器時代住居跡（市指定文化財）が移設される{{sfn|八王子市}}。 *{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）菅公1075年忌事業により社殿を銅板葺きに改修する。 ===御神木=== {{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}に植えられたとされるケヤキは現代でも境内にある。 2010年代から天満社外周のケヤキやサクラなどの枝が道路にはみ出すなどしているため、市から剪定するように{{jalink|行政指導|Q4683583}}を受け、 2019年12月には{{jalink|国土交通省|Q1376196}}からも当月中に剪定するよう行政指導を受けているが、応じる気配がない[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 信号機も隠す危ない「ご神木」　撤去しない神社の言い分] - {{jalink|朝日新聞|Q720503}}。 2020年6月20日に国土交通省相武国道事務所と八王子市が行政代執行法に基づき、はみ出た枝を強制的に伐採した{{Citenews|title=八王子市と国、代執行でご神木伐 周辺道路に枝葉、指導22回も神社側応じず|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=毎日新聞|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}。 === 文化財 === ; 北野石器時代住居跡: 八王子市指定史跡・旧跡{{sfn|八王子市}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|京王線|Q1323112}} {{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}} 北口 徒歩3分{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|八王子バイパス|Q11391552}} {{jalink|打越インターチェンジ|Q11497261|打越IC}} === 塩竃神社 === {{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753}}とも。腹帯神社として有名である。天満社の右側に並んでいる。 {{節スタブ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=天満社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=北野石器時代住居跡|publisher=八王子市|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === 関連文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ100多磨郡ノ12|chapter=北野村 天満宮|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]]

{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||塩土老翁}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（天満社） |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 北野天神・北野天満宮・天神さんなど |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}}付近（天満社）{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = {{jalink|7月26日|Q2723}}付近（鹽竈神社） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=天満社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''天満社'''（てんまんしゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}にある神社。{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}の地名の由来になっている。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、ここでは末社の{{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753|塩竃神社}}も説明する。 === 歴史 === *創建は不明、{{jalink|横山党|Q11542517}}の一族が{{jalink|北野天満宮|Q662176|京都北野天満宮}}を{{jalink|勧請}}したとされている『八王子長沼町・北野町わが街』 清水正之著 温故堂 1987年発行『八王子辞典』 八王子辞典の会 かたくら書店 2001年発行。 *{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}（{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年）〜{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}（{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年）5石の{{jalink|社領}}を拝領する{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}将軍に武運長久の祈願札を毎年献上していた{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}ご神木である{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}が植えられる。 *{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年）社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}13年）{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}向拝の社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）{{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}}周辺の区画整理により{{jalink|八王子市立由井第一小学校|Q11391597|由井第一小学校}}内にあった北野石器時代住居跡（市指定文化財）が移設される{{sfn|八王子市}}。 *{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）菅公1075年忌事業により社殿を銅板葺きに改修する。 ===御神木=== {{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}に植えられたとされるケヤキは現代でも境内にある。 2010年代から天満社外周のケヤキやサクラなどの枝が道路にはみ出すなどしているため、市から剪定するように{{jalink|行政指導|Q4683583}}を受け、 2019年12月には{{jalink|国土交通省|Q1376196}}からも当月中に剪定するよう行政指導を受けているが、応じる気配がない[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 信号機も隠す危ない「ご神木」　撤去しない神社の言い分] - {{jalink|朝日新聞|Q720503}}。 2020年6月20日に国土交通省相武国道事務所と八王子市が行政代執行法に基づき、はみ出た枝を強制的に伐採した{{Citenews|title=八王子市と国、代執行でご神木伐 周辺道路に枝葉、指導22回も神社側応じず|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=毎日新聞|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}。 === 文化財 === ; 北野石器時代住居跡: 八王子市指定史跡・旧跡{{sfn|八王子市}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|京王線|Q1323112}} {{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}} 北口 徒歩3分{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|八王子バイパス|Q11391552}} {{jalink|打越インターチェンジ|Q11497261|打越IC}} === 塩竃神社 === {{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753}}とも。腹帯神社として有名である。天満社の右側に並んでいる。 {{節スタブ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=天満社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=北野石器時代住居跡|publisher=八王子市|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === 関連文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ100多磨郡ノ12|chapter=北野村 天満宮|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 天満社・鹽竈神社 |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
天満社 |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}550番地1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''天満社'''
{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''鹽竈神社'''
{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||塩土老翁}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（天満社） |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 北野天神・北野天満宮・天神さんなど |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}}付近（天満社）{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = {{jalink|7月26日|Q2723}}付近（鹽竈神社） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=天満社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''天満社'''（てんまんしゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}にある神社。{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}の地名の由来になっている。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、ここでは末社の{{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753|塩竃神社}}も説明する。 === 歴史 === *創建は不明、{{jalink|横山党|Q11542517}}の一族が{{jalink|北野天満宮|Q662176|京都北野天満宮}}を{{jalink|勧請}}したとされている『八王子長沼町・北野町わが街』 清水正之著 温故堂 1987年発行『八王子辞典』 八王子辞典の会 かたくら書店 2001年発行。 *{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}（{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年）〜{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}（{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年）5石の{{jalink|社領}}を拝領する{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}将軍に武運長久の祈願札を毎年献上していた{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}ご神木である{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}が植えられる。 *{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年）社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}13年）{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}向拝の社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）{{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}}周辺の区画整理により{{jalink|八王子市立由井第一小学校|Q11391597|由井第一小学校}}内にあった北野石器時代住居跡（市指定文化財）が移設される{{sfn|八王子市}}。 *{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）菅公1075年忌事業により社殿を銅板葺きに改修する。 ===御神木=== {{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}に植えられたとされるケヤキは現代でも境内にある。 2010年代から天満社外周のケヤキやサクラなどの枝が道路にはみ出すなどしているため、市から剪定するように{{jalink|行政指導|Q4683583}}を受け、 2019年12月には{{jalink|国土交通省|Q1376196}}からも当月中に剪定するよう行政指導を受けているが、応じる気配がない[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 信号機も隠す危ない「ご神木」　撤去しない神社の言い分] - {{jalink|朝日新聞|Q720503}}。 2020年6月20日に国土交通省相武国道事務所と八王子市が行政代執行法に基づき、はみ出た枝を強制的に伐採した{{Citenews|title=八王子市と国、代執行でご神木伐 周辺道路に枝葉、指導22回も神社側応じず|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=毎日新聞|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}。 === 文化財 === ; 北野石器時代住居跡: 八王子市指定史跡・旧跡{{sfn|八王子市}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|京王線|Q1323112}} {{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}} 北口 徒歩3分{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|八王子バイパス|Q11391552}} {{jalink|打越インターチェンジ|Q11497261|打越IC}} === 塩竃神社 === {{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753}}とも。腹帯神社として有名である。天満社の右側に並んでいる。 {{節スタブ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=天満社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=北野石器時代住居跡|publisher=八王子市|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === 関連文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ100多磨郡ノ12|chapter=北野村 天満宮|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 天満社・鹽竈神社 |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
天満社 |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}550番地1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''天満社'''
{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''鹽竈神社'''
{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||塩土老翁}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（天満社） |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 北野天神・北野天満宮・天神さんなど |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}}付近（天満社）{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = {{jalink|7月26日|Q2723}}付近（鹽竈神社） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=天満社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''天満社'''（てんまんしゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}にある神社。{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}の地名の由来になっている。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、ここでは末社の{{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753|塩竃神社}}も説明する。 === 歴史 === *創建は不明、{{jalink|横山党|Q11542517}}の一族が{{jalink|北野天満宮|Q662176|京都北野天満宮}}を{{jalink|勧請}}したとされている『八王子長沼町・北野町わが街』 清水正之著 温故堂 1987年発行『八王子辞典』 八王子辞典の会 かたくら書店 2001年発行。 *{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}（{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年）〜{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}（{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年）5石の{{jalink|社領}}を拝領する{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}将軍に武運長久の祈願札を毎年献上していた{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}ご神木である{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}が植えられる。 *{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年）社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}13年）{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}向拝の社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）{{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}}周辺の区画整理により{{jalink|八王子市立由井第一小学校|Q11391597|由井第一小学校}}内にあった北野石器時代住居跡（市指定文化財）が移設される{{sfn|八王子市}}。 *{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）菅公1075年忌事業により社殿を銅板葺きに改修する。 ===御神木=== {{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}に植えられたとされるケヤキは現代でも境内にある。 2010年代から天満社外周のケヤキやサクラなどの枝が道路にはみ出すなどしているため、市から剪定するように{{jalink|行政指導|Q4683583}}を受け、 2019年12月には{{jalink|国土交通省|Q1376196}}からも当月中に剪定するよう行政指導を受けているが、応じる気配がない[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 信号機も隠す危ない「ご神木」　撤去しない神社の言い分] - {{jalink|朝日新聞|Q720503}}。 2020年6月20日に国土交通省相武国道事務所と八王子市が行政代執行法に基づき、はみ出た枝を強制的に伐採した{{Citenews|title=八王子市と国、代執行でご神木伐 周辺道路に枝葉、指導22回も神社側応じず|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=毎日新聞|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}。 === 文化財 === ; 北野石器時代住居跡: 八王子市指定史跡・旧跡{{sfn|八王子市}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|京王線|Q1323112}} {{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}} 北口 徒歩3分{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|八王子バイパス|Q11391552}} {{jalink|打越インターチェンジ|Q11497261|打越IC}} === 塩竃神社 === {{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753}}とも。腹帯神社として有名である。天満社の右側に並んでいる。 {{節スタブ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=天満社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=北野石器時代住居跡|publisher=八王子市|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === 関連文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ100多磨郡ノ12|chapter=北野村 天満宮|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 天満社・鹽竈神社 |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
天満社 |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}550番地1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''天満社'''
{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''鹽竈神社'''
{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||塩土老翁}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（天満社） |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 北野天神・北野天満宮・天神さんなど |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}}付近（天満社）{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = {{jalink|7月26日|Q2723}}付近（鹽竈神社） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=天満社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''天満社'''（てんまんしゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}にある神社。{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}の地名の由来になっている。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、ここでは末社の{{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753|塩竃神社}}も説明する。 === 歴史 === *創建は不明、{{jalink|横山党|Q11542517}}の一族が{{jalink|北野天満宮|Q662176|京都北野天満宮}}を{{jalink|勧請}}したとされている『八王子長沼町・北野町わが街』 清水正之著 温故堂 1987年発行『八王子辞典』 八王子辞典の会 かたくら書店 2001年発行。 *{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}（{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年）〜{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}（{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年）5石の{{jalink|社領}}を拝領する{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}将軍に武運長久の祈願札を毎年献上していた{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}ご神木である{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}が植えられる。 *{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年）社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}13年）{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}向拝の社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）{{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}}周辺の区画整理により{{jalink|八王子市立由井第一小学校|Q11391597|由井第一小学校}}内にあった北野石器時代住居跡（市指定文化財）が移設される{{sfn|八王子市}}。 *{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）菅公1075年忌事業により社殿を銅板葺きに改修する。 ===御神木=== {{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}に植えられたとされるケヤキは現代でも境内にある。 2010年代から天満社外周のケヤキやサクラなどの枝が道路にはみ出すなどしているため、市から剪定するように{{jalink|行政指導|Q4683583}}を受け、 2019年12月には{{jalink|国土交通省|Q1376196}}からも当月中に剪定するよう行政指導を受けているが、応じる気配がない[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 信号機も隠す危ない「ご神木」　撤去しない神社の言い分] - {{jalink|朝日新聞|Q720503}}。 2020年6月20日に国土交通省相武国道事務所と八王子市が行政代執行法に基づき、はみ出た枝を強制的に伐採した{{Citenews|title=八王子市と国、代執行でご神木伐 周辺道路に枝葉、指導22回も神社側応じず|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=毎日新聞|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}。 === 文化財 === ; 北野石器時代住居跡: 八王子市指定史跡・旧跡{{sfn|八王子市}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|京王線|Q1323112}} {{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}} 北口 徒歩3分{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|八王子バイパス|Q11391552}} {{jalink|打越インターチェンジ|Q11497261|打越IC}} === 塩竃神社 === {{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753}}とも。腹帯神社として有名である。天満社の右側に並んでいる。 {{節スタブ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=天満社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=北野石器時代住居跡|publisher=八王子市|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === 関連文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ100多磨郡ノ12|chapter=北野村 天満宮|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武甕槌命}}・{{jalink|シオツチノオジ||塩土老翁}}・{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}（天満社） |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = 北野天神・北野天満宮・天神さんなど |例祭 = {{jalink|4月25日|Q2531}}付近（天満社）{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = {{jalink|7月26日|Q2723}}付近（鹽竈神社） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=天満社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''天満社'''（てんまんしゃ）は、{{jalink|東京都|Q1490}}{{jalink|八王子市|Q208863}}にある神社。{{jalink|北野町 (八王子市)|Q11404303|北野町}}の地名の由来になっている。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}、ここでは末社の{{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753|塩竃神社}}も説明する。 === 歴史 === *創建は不明、{{jalink|横山党|Q11542517}}の一族が{{jalink|北野天満宮|Q662176|京都北野天満宮}}を{{jalink|勧請}}したとされている『八王子長沼町・北野町わが街』 清水正之著 温故堂 1987年発行『八王子辞典』 八王子辞典の会 かたくら書店 2001年発行。 *{{jalink|1591年|Q6766}}（{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}19年）〜{{jalink|1860年|Q7691}}（{{jalink|万延|Q1193862}}元年）5石の{{jalink|社領}}を拝領する{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}将軍に武運長久の祈願札を毎年献上していた{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}。 *{{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}ご神木である{{jalink|ケヤキ|Q920344}}が植えられる。 *{{jalink|1664年|Q7581}}（{{jalink|寛文|Q1146228}}4年）社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}13年）{{jalink|唐破風|Q977293}}向拝の社殿を再建する。 *{{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）{{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}}周辺の区画整理により{{jalink|八王子市立由井第一小学校|Q11391597|由井第一小学校}}内にあった北野石器時代住居跡（市指定文化財）が移設される{{sfn|八王子市}}。 *{{jalink|1976年|Q2480}}（昭和51年）菅公1075年忌事業により社殿を銅板葺きに改修する。 ===御神木=== {{jalink|1600年代|Q42995}}に植えられたとされるケヤキは現代でも境内にある。 2010年代から天満社外周のケヤキやサクラなどの枝が道路にはみ出すなどしているため、市から剪定するように{{jalink|行政指導|Q4683583}}を受け、 2019年12月には{{jalink|国土交通省|Q1376196}}からも当月中に剪定するよう行政指導を受けているが、応じる気配がない[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 信号機も隠す危ない「ご神木」　撤去しない神社の言い分] - {{jalink|朝日新聞|Q720503}}。 2020年6月20日に国土交通省相武国道事務所と八王子市が行政代執行法に基づき、はみ出た枝を強制的に伐採した{{Citenews|title=八王子市と国、代執行でご神木伐 周辺道路に枝葉、指導22回も神社側応じず|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=毎日新聞|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}。 === 文化財 === ; 北野石器時代住居跡: 八王子市指定史跡・旧跡{{sfn|八王子市}} === 交通 === * {{jalink|京王線|Q1323112}} {{jalink|北野駅 (東京都)|Q4222444|北野駅}} 北口 徒歩3分{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|八王子バイパス|Q11391552}} {{jalink|打越インターチェンジ|Q11497261|打越IC}} === 塩竃神社 === {{jalink|鹽竈神社|Q133753}}とも。腹帯神社として有名である。天満社の右側に並んでいる。 {{節スタブ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=天満社|publisher=東京都神社庁|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=北野石器時代住居跡|publisher=八王子市|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === 関連文献 === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|新編武蔵風土記稿|Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ100多磨郡ノ12|chapter=北野村 天満宮|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|天満宮|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}{{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}{{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}{{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}{{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}{{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]]

'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{jalink|Tokyo|Q1490}}{{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}{{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{jalink|Shiotsuchi no Oji||Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} |社格 = {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{jalink|April 25|Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{jalink|July 26|Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{jalink|Hachiōji|Q208863}}, {{jalink|Tokyo}}. It is the origin of the place name {{jalink|Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{jalink|Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517}} invited {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{jalink|1591|Q6766}} ({{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 19) to {{jalink|1860|Q7691}} ({{jalink|Man'en|Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{jalink|sharyō}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}}, the sacred {{jalink|zelkova|Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{jalink|1664|Q7581}} ({{jalink|Kanbun|Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{jalink|karahafu|Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{jalink|Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{jalink|1976|Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{jalink|1600s|Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{jalink|administrative guidance|Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{jalink|Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{jalink|Asahi Shimbun|Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{jalink|Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|Kitano Station}} on the {{jalink|Keiō Line|Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{jalink|Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552}} {{jalink|Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{jalink|Shiogama Shrine|Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{jalink|Tenmangū|Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji||WD=|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill||lt=Tokyo|UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill||lt=sharyō|UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji||WD=|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
