* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji||WD=|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|lt=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|lt=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181695 ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '〒192-0906 東京都八王子市北野町550番地1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '東京都青梅市一御岳山', 'language': 'ja'} ** P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|ltq=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|ltq=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517|1=Yokoyama-tō}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581|1=1664}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School|1=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583|1=Administrative guidance}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552|1=Hachioji bypass}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC|1=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|1=Shiogama shrine}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517|1=Yokoyama-tō}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581|1=1664}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School|1=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583|1=Administrative guidance}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552|1=Hachioji bypass}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC|1=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|1=Shiogama shrine}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517|1=Yokoyama-tō}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581|1=1664}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School|1=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583|1=Administrative guidance}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552|1=Hachioji bypass}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC|1=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|1=Shiogama shrine}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんまんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]]

'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517|1=Yokoyama-tō}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581|1=1664}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School|1=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583|1=Administrative guidance}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552|1=Hachioji bypass}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC|1=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|1=Shiogama shrine}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]][[Category:qq]]

P2241: {{ill|Q41755623|ltq=Q41755623|WD=Q41755623|1=incorrect value}} ** P8327: {{ill|Q6587276|ltq=Q6587276|WD=Q6587276|1=Musashi-Mitake Shrine}} [[JA:天満社 (八王子市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11442850}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tenman-sha / Shiogama Shrine |画像 = [[画像:北野天満社.jpg|260px]]
Tenman-sha |所在地 = 〒192-0906
{{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}{{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}550-1{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = '''Tenman-sha'''
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}
'''Shiogama Shrine'''
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}・{{ill|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiotsuchi no Oji}}・{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} (Tenman-sha) |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Kitano Tenjin / Kitano Tenmangū / Tenjin-san etc. |例祭 = Near {{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} (Tenman-sha){{sfn|東京都神社庁}} |神事 = Near {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} (Shiogama Shrine) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|label=Tenman-sha|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tenman-sha''' (てんまんしゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hachiōji|Q208863|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tokyo|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is the origin of the place name {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō (Hachiōji)|Q11404303|WD=Q11404303|lt=Kitano-chō|1=Kitanomachi}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; this article also explains the subordinate {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}}. === History === * The founding is unknown; it is said a branch of the {{ill|lt=Yokoyama-tō|Q11542517|WD=Q11542517|1=Yokoyama-tō}} invited {{ill|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|WD=Q662176|lt=Kyoto Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} here'Hachiōji Naganuma-chō, Kitano-chō Wagamachi,' Masayuki Shimizu, Onkodō, 1987.'Hachiōji Jiten,' Hachiōji Jiten no Kai, Katakura Shoten, 2001.. * From {{ill|lt=1591|Q6766|WD=Q6766|1=1591}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19) to {{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}} ({{ill|lt=Man'en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1), it received 5 koku of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sharyō|1=UNKNOWN}} land{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, prayer talismans for the shogun's military fortune were offered every year{{sfn|東京都神社庁}}. * In the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}}, the sacred {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree was planted. * In {{ill|lt=1664|Q7581|WD=Q7581|1=1664}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 4), shrine buildings were rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 13), the shrine building with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|WD=Q977293|1=Karahafu}}-fronted portico was rebuilt. * In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 38), during land readjustment around {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}}, the Kitano Stone Age dwelling site (city-designated cultural property), formerly on grounds of {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Municipal Yui Daiichi Elementary School|Q11391597|WD=Q11391597|lt=Yui Daiichi Elementary School|1=Yui Daiichi Elementary School}}, was relocated{{sfn|八王子市}}. * In {{ill|lt=1976|Q2480|WD=Q2480|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), for the 1075th memorial of Kankō (Michizane), the shrine roof was renovated with copper sheeting. === Sacred tree === The zelkova said to have been planted in the {{ill|lt=1600s|Q42995|WD=Q42995|1=1600s (decade)}} still stands in the precincts. Since the 2010s, branches of zelkova and cherry trees around the shrine perimeter extended over roads, and the city repeatedly gave {{ill|lt=administrative guidance|Q4683583|WD=Q4683583|1=Administrative guidance}} to prune them. In December 2019, the {{ill|lt=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism|Q1376196|WD=Q1376196|1=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism}} also instructed pruning within that month, but there were no signs of compliance[https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASM9N3TFVM9NUTIL010.html?iref=com_rnavi_arank_nd04 Dangerous "sacred tree" even hides traffic lights: shrine's explanation for not removing it] - {{ill|lt=Asahi Shimbun|Q720503|WD=Q720503|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}. On June 20, 2020, the MLIT Sōbu National Highway Office and Hachiōji City, under the Administrative Execution Act, forcibly cut protruding branches{{Citenews|title=Hachiōji City and the state conduct administrative execution to cut sacred tree: branches/leaves over roads, shrine ignored 22 rounds of guidance|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20200621/k00/00m/040/020000c|newspaper=Mainichi Shimbun|date=2020-06-21|accessdate=2020-06-21}}. === Cultural property === ; Kitano Stone Age dwelling site: Hachiōji city-designated historic site / old site{{sfn|八王子市}} === Transport === * 3 minutes on foot from north exit of {{ill|lt=Kitano Station (Tokyo)|Q4222444|WD=Q4222444|lt=Kitano Station|1=Kitano Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|lt=Keiō Line|Q1323112|WD=Q1323112|1=Keiō Line}}{{sfn|東京都神社庁}} * {{ill|lt=Hachiōji Bypass|Q11391552|WD=Q11391552|1=Hachioji bypass}} {{ill|lt=Uchikoshi Interchange|Q11497261|WD=Q11497261|lt=Uchikoshi IC|1=Uchikoshi IC}} === Shiogama Shrine === Also called {{ill|lt=Shiogama Shrine|Q133753|WD=Q133753|1=Shiogama shrine}}. It is famous as a shrine for abdominal-belt prayers for safe childbirth. It stands to the right of Tenman-sha. {{節スタブ}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/5793/|title=Tenman-sha|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-06-18|ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hachioji.tokyo.jp/kankobunka/003/003/001/003/p005274.html|title=Kitano Stone Age dwelling site|publisher=Hachiōji City|accessdate=2020-06-18|date=2016-06-29|ref=harv}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol.100 Tama District 12|chapter=Kitano Village Tenmangū|id={{NDLJP|763990/49}}|publisher=Home Ministry Geography Bureau|date=1884-6}} === Related item === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tenmansha]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tenmansha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:東京都の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:塩竈神社]]
